Input: Today, I wake up at 07.00a.m and catch up some of kind bread with jam, drinks a coffee but more than often just has time for half mug and runs to work.
Output: Today, I wake up at 07.00am and grab some kind of bread with jam, drink a coffee, but more often than not, just have time for a half mug and run to work.
Updated Feedback: Great job using present simple for daily routines! To improve clarity and grammar: Use "07:00am" (no space), "grab" instead of "catch up," and "kind of" for word order. For subject-verb agreement, use "drink" and "have/run." Also, "more often than not" is the correct idiom. Keep practicing your routine descriptions—they’re very useful!

Input: During a day I have lunch which is from can or order some junky food.
Output: During the day, I have lunch, which is from a can or order some junk food.
Updated Feedback: Good start describing your daily meals! Remember to use "the day" and "a can" for articles. "Junky" should be "junk." Try to add more details about your lunch—what do you usually order?

Input: Later, I order take away from Chinese buffet or any pizza.

Updated Feedback: Well done using present simple! "Takeaway" is one word, and don’t forget articles: "a Chinese buffet" and "a pizza." What’s your favorite pizza topping?

Input: When weekend coming I have a plan for special dinner which is like home made but do n't have time again.
Output: When the weekend comes, I have a plan for a special dinner, which is like homemade, but I don’t have time again.
Updated Feedback: Excellent use of commas for clauses! Use "the weekend comes" for subject-verb agreement, "homemade" as one word, and "don’t" for contractions. Try to explain why you don’t have time—it helps with fluency!

Input: What happened with my healthy life which I want to have when I was a child?
